title: galium × ochroleucum var. bailletii
intro: Galium × ochroleucum var. bailletii, also known as the French or Bailey's wood sorrel, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its astringent and antiseptic qualities, which can be attributed to its high concentration of tannins and flavonoids. The plant's health benefits include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antimicrobial agent, making it a popular choice for treating various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Galium × ochroleucum var. bailletii can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support the body's natural healing processes and promote overall well-being.
